story.title: Google Login
story.user_story: As a returning user, I want to sign in with Google so that I can access my account quickly without entering a password.
acceptance_criteria: Given the user initiates the login process via the frontend, when the backend receives the OAuth callback from Google, then the email is mapped to an existing user or a pending user record is created.
Given the requirement is approved, when planning starts, then the story is documented clearly.
Given the team reviews the story, when acceptance criteria are checked, then at least 3 criteria are present.

be: Map the Google email to an existing user or create a pending user record upon successful OAuth callback.
Handle the case where the user cancels or rejects the login flow by displaying a clear error message.
Store the access token securely and redirect the user to authenticated routes.
fe: Show a clear error message if Google authentication is cancelled or rejected.
Display the frontend UI for storing the access token securely after successful login.
Redirect the user to the authenticated routes upon successful login with Google.
qa: Test the happy path scenario where the user successfully logs in with Google and stores the access token securely.
Test the failure path scenarios where the user cancels or rejects the login flow, ensuring a clear error message is displayed.
Ensure that the frontend UI for storing the access token securely after successful login works as expected.

definition_of_done: Story is ready for Jira creation.
assumptions: The backend will have a mechanism to validate and map the Google email to an existing user or create a pending user record.
warnings: Planner returned clarification questions for READY story; removed inconsistent questions.
